title: Stint Hazard Features From Empirical Pit Windows
summary: F1 strategy models frame pit timing around optimal stint length, pit-loss tradeoffs, and compound-specific degradation.
rationale: F1 strategy models frame pit timing around optimal stint length, pit-loss tradeoffs, and compound-specific degradation. Instead of only raw TyreLife interactions, encode how close a row is to historically typical stop ages for that Race/Compound/Stint/Year bucket.
implementation_hint: Create out-of-fold aggregates on training rows where PitNextLap=1: median/quantile TyreLife and LapNumber by Race, Compound, Stint, Year and fallback buckets. Add distance_to_median_pit_age, pct_of_pit_window, above_q75_window, and hazard_prior features; train GBM/CatBoost on these features.
expected_effect: Improves rank ordering near likely pit windows, especially for common one-stop/two-stop strategy patterns.
risk: If computed without OOF discipline, target encodings leak. Sparse buckets need smoothing/fallbacks.
